LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-17-2023 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MESEKA HENRY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT HENRY'S DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER IS FILED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND HENRY'S RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY FEBRUARY 17, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)
ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-18-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Continued To Jun-13-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MESEKA HENRY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend.
The parties agree that the "the subject accident occurred on 8/19/19," that "the regular two year statute" of limitation applied and that the complaint "was filed on February 16, 2022." (Opp. 1:16-20.)
The parties disagree about the length of the tolling period afforded by Covid-19 Emergency Rule 9 - plaintiff says it was "180 days," defendant "178 days." (Opp. 1:16; Memo. 1:20.) The emergency rule's pertinent part reads: "Notwithstanding any other laws, the statutes of limitation and repose for civil causes of action that exceed 180 days are tolled from April 6, 2020, until October 1, 2020."
Defendant is correct: April 6 until October 1 is 178 days. The rule's "180 days" refers to statutes of limitations, not the tolling period. Plaintiff cites no authority to the contrary. Two years plus 178 days from August 19, 2019 was February 13, 2022 (a Sunday); plaintiff's February 16, 2022 complaint was not timely filed.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2023 Continued To Apr-11-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MESEKA HENRY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Henry's demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Henry's response to the complaint is now due by February 17, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES: Rafael Crespo, Jr. Esq. appearing for plaintiff. The OSC is continued to Feb-7-2023. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; Clerk: JLee; Proceedings not reported.
